action.reasoning: Observation: Iter_8 achieved 49.6% success rate (new best). Four environments remain at 0% (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom) and FourRooms is only 24%. These are large, sparse-reward environments requiring long-horizon memory and exploration. Analysis: The current frame stack of 4 provides only ~4 steps of memory, insufficient for multi-room navigation. Additionally, I need to ensure SB3 does not normalize image observations before they reach my embedding-based extractor (normalize_images=False). To improve exploration in hard tasks, I will: 1) increase frame_stack to 8 for more temporal context, 2) raise entropy coefficient to 0.03, 3) double n_steps to 8192 for lower-variance updates on longer episodes, 4) increase batch_size to 1024, 5) set gamma=0.995 for longer credit assignment, 6) train for 30M steps (~4h). Decision: Update train.py, predict.py, and wrappers.py with these hyperparameters and submit a long training run.
